channel.title: +Persons on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Persons on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Baji Rout
item.description: a boy from Nilakanthpur in Orissa, who refused to ferry murderous British soldiers across the brAhmaNI river despite blows, and died for it. [TW]

item.title: bhIm rAv
item.description: ambeDkar Strived for respect and opportunities for the untouchable jAti-s - even at the cost of hinduism or Indian independence, an intelligent subversive inimical to hindus, used Christian categories to accomplish this (JDRoover here). As such he was a class opportunist. In 1942 Dr.Ambedkar’s Golden Jubilee was celebrated. The function was presided over by Dr.M.R.Jayakar, a Hindu Maha Sabha leader. On this occasion, Veer Savarkar felicitated Dr.Ambedkar thus: Ambedkar’s personality, erudition and capacity to lead and organize would have by themselves marked him out as an outstanding asset to our nation.

item.title: shAlUmarada timakka and her husband bikkuLu chikkaNNa
item.description: a childless couple who happily fostered 284+ trees, watered and fed cattle, lived a happy life, refused “casteist” awards [ST, TW].

item.title: Zumbarbai Aausak and her cohort from sangamner
item.description: who defied British police to start rathayAtrA [SW].
